title: Entertainment Licensing Reform -- 350-Person Threshold, County Planning Board Referral Exemption
summary: Jake Dishaw (Deputy Commissioner Code Enforcement) presents item 52, entertainment licensing ordinance reform creating two license types based on 350-person crowd threshold. Large venues (e.g., Westcott Theater) need per-event licenses for emergency response review; small venues (e.g., 443 Social) get annual licenses. Modeled on certificate of use and tobacco licensing with revocation procedures. Responds to business owner feedback about over-regulation. Item 53: exempting certain low-impact zoning actions (variances, special permits, site plans) from mandatory referral to Onondaga County Planning Board, reducing delays caused by OCPB schedule misalignment. Larger projects still referred; aims to eliminate red tape for low-impact development.

processing_notes: 350-person threshold separates annual vs. per-event licensing. Key driver is public safety/emergency response planning for large gatherings. OCPB = Onondaga County Planning Board. Law Department detection is incidental to general municipal law discussion. Entertainment licensing reform directly responds to constituent and business community feedback. Session adjourns after this segment -- executive session deferred to Monday.
